observation: Symptom: `agent-bober` CLI aborts with `ReferenceError: Cannot access 'DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS' before initialization` at dist/fleet/critic-deep.js:7
Confirmed CURRENT after a fresh `npm run build` (tsc clean). Crash reproduces on `--help`, `--version`, and `onboard` — 1 TDZ line each.
Scope: GLOBAL. Crash occurs at CLI import/module-load time, not inside any command handler — `import('./dist/cli/index.js')` alone throws. Every agent-bober command is dead.
Timing: introduced by commit e4f7b6b 'critique engine (critic-deep.ts) + opt-in threading' (fleet Phase 4) on branch bober/medical-team.

BOUNDARY A (static import graph): cycle decomposer-deep <-> critic-deep. decomposer-deep.ts:5 imports runCritiqueLoop from critic-deep; critic-deep.ts:4-9 imports DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS + DEEP_EXPAND_MAX_RETRIES from decomposer-deep AND reads them at module init (critic-deep.ts:16-18).
BOUNDARY A': CLI reaches the cycle via src/fleet/index.ts:27 `import { decomposeGoalDeep } from ./decomposer-deep.js` — i.e. decomposer-deep is loaded BEFORE critic-deep (the crashing order).
BOUNDARY B (runtime ESM loader): native Node stack shows crash at critic-deep.js:7 inside ModuleJob.run (module-evaluation phase), confirming module-init TDZ rather than a call-time logic error.

BOUNDARY C (differential): `vitest run critic-deep.test.ts decomposer-deep.test.ts` => 73/73 PASS. Tests are green despite the cycle because the vitest loader hits the safe import order; native ESM CLI hits the crashing order. Classic passes-tests/breaks-binary.

DISPROOF (load-order test): `import('dist/fleet/critic-deep.js')` FIRST => OK, DEEP_CRITIQUE_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS = 8. `import('dist/fleet/decomposer-deep.js')` FIRST => CRASH (exact error). Order-dependency proven; rules out stale-dist, logic-bug, and always-undefined alternatives.
